after_heading: 2. Queries, Keys, Values, and Scaled Dot-Product Attention
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a row of expressive characters seated along a glowing horizontal timeline track, where one highlighted figure in the middle beams curved rays of light toward every other figure at once, each ray drawn at a different thickness to show how strongly that neighbor matters, the thickest beams reaching a couple of important figures and the faintest barely touching the rest. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD or Kurzgesagt panel, expressive faces, one clear visual idea, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. alt: One highlighted character on a timeline beams attention rays of varying thickness to every other character at once, thick beams to the few that matter and faint beams to the rest, picturing how a query scores every key and reads a weighted blend of values.
caption: Attention lets a position consult the entire sequence at once and listen loudest to whoever matters most, no waiting and no fading.

after_heading: 1. The Encoder Block: Attention, FFN, Residual, and Layer Normalization
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a tall cutaway building made of several identical stacked floors, each floor split into two rooms, a round social room where little figures all turn to face and talk to one another and a tidy workshop room where a single figure tinkers alone at a bench, and running up the outside of the building a set of glowing skip-elevators that carry copies of the figures straight past a floor and rejoin them at the top. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever Kurzgesagt cross-section, expressive figures, clean architectural lines, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A cutaway tower of identical floors, each with a round mingling room for self-attention and a solo workshop for the feedforward network, with glowing skip-elevators on the outside carrying signals past each floor, picturing a stack of Transformer blocks with residual connections.
caption: A Transformer is the same two-room floor stacked many times over, and the residual skip-elevators are what keep the deep stack from losing its footing.

after_heading: 1. Why Attention Is Order-Blind: Permutation Equivariance
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a theater row of identical seats arranged left to right, where a crowd of look-alike token characters who clearly have no idea where they belong are each being gently guided into a specific seat along the row by a friendly usher, so their left-to-right seat position alone now tells them their order, the front-row seats nearest the viewer and the back ones receding. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD panel, expressive confused-then-relieved faces, position conveyed purely by seat placement, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: Identical order-blind token characters are each ushered into a specific seat along a single theater row, so their left-to-right seat position alone encodes their place in the sequence, picturing positional encoding restoring order to permutation-invariant attention.
caption: Attention cannot tell who came first, so positional encoding gives every token an assigned seat and lets its place in the row carry the order.

after_heading: 1. The Quadratic Wall: Why Attention Costs $O(L^2)$
prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: a cramped reception room where every single guest insists on shaking hands with every other guest at the same time, their arms tangling into an exploding web of crisscrossing handshakes; one small cluster of guests is calm and orderly while a much larger crowd has dissolved into an overwhelming knot of arms reaching everywhere, the floor visibly running out of room as more guests pour in through a doorway. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD panel, expressive overwhelmed faces, one clear visual gag about all-to-all blowing up, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A reception room where every guest shakes hands with every other guest at once, a small calm cluster beside a much larger crowd whose handshakes explode into a tangled web as more guests arrive, picturing how all-to-all attention cost grows as the square of sequence length.
caption: When everyone must greet everyone, adding a few more guests makes the handshakes explode, the quadratic cost that walls off long sequences.

prompt: A witty cartoon-like educational illustration with clean lines and a warm color palette: the same reception room as before but now calm and uncluttered, where each guest shakes hands only with a couple of close neighbors plus one or two far-off guests across the room, the few deliberate handshakes drawn as clean uncrossed lines so the room breathes with open space instead of a tangle, the guests relaxed and purposeful. Style: friendly and approachable like a clever XKCD panel, expressive content faces, one clear visual contrast of selective versus all-to-all, generous negative space, suitable for a technical textbook. No text, letters, numbers, or labels anywhere in the image.
alt: A calm reception room where each guest shakes hands only with a few near neighbors and one or two distant guests, the sparse clean connections leaving the room uncluttered, picturing sparse and efficient attention that replaces all-to-all comparison with a selective few.
caption: Efficient attention keeps the conversation by letting each token greet only a chosen few, trading the exploding all-to-all web for a handful of well-placed handshakes.
